steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content:
The sentence contains two independent clauses: the first ending after “Mohegan [blank]” describes the museum's 1931 founding with its goal, and the second beginning with “today, nearly a century later” describes what it is now.

The second clause has “it” as subject and “is the oldest” as the main verb.

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses require a comma plus a coordinating conjunction to join correctly.

Choice A uses Tribe, and—the comma precedes “and,” which properly joins the clauses.

Choices B and D create comma splices (joining with only a comma), and C uses “and” without the required comma.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
